payload.body:
## Merge-ready handoff

I’ll trace the three existing definitions and package boundaries first, then consolidate them into the narrowest canonical owner and run the affected typechecks/tests. I’ll preserve existing encoded values and public behavior.The ticket’s desktop path is stale in this worktree—the referenced file no longer exists there. I’m locating its current owner and checking whether part of the consolidation has already happened, so the change lands in the package that actually owns these contracts.I found the clean ownership point: `@cycle/contracts/schemas/app`. I’m introducing a focused `AppSettings` contract there, then making config parsing, API schemas/runtime types, backend services, and desktop mutations consume those exact schemas/types. This also removes the remaining hand-written API unions without changing any accepted values.The duplicate literal scan found four additional API-host unions outside the ticket’s original evidence (server, MCP runtime/CLI, and backend options). I’m folding those into the same canonical `ApiHost` type as well; otherwise the “one literal set” criterion would still be only partially true.The consolidation is now structurally complete: the four literal families appear only in `AppSettings.ts`, and runtime host guards use the schema-derived predicate. I’m moving into verification now—package typechecks first, then focused config/API/desktop tests and formatting.Typechecks couldn’t start because this prepared worktree has no installed dependencies (`node_modules` is absent); there are no code diagnostics yet. I’m installing the locked workspace dependencies, then I’ll rerun the same checks.The focused config and contract tests pass. API and one desktop integration suite are blocked before test collection by an existing MCP module initialization error (`registerCycleMcpResources` used before declaration), matching the unrelated typecheck failure. I’m adding a direct API contract test so the changed HTTP payload/output schemas are still exercised independently of that broken startup path.The isolated HTTP schema tests pass, and all changed TypeScript files are clean under lint and formatting. I’m doing the final diff review now, including checking that no old config imports or duplicated unions remain and that package metadata only reflects the new contracts dependency.Implemented UKN-8X352.

### Completed

- Added canonical schemas/types for API host, theme, density, and commit style in [AppSettings.ts](/Users/robertpitt/.cycle/worktrees/repo_16371/worktree_mrkou2gd_iiygjjib45br/packages/contracts/src/schemas/app/AppSettings.ts:1).
- Migrated config parsing/defaults, API payload/output schemas, runtime provider types, server/MCP host validation, backend services, and desktop settings consumers to the canonical contracts.
- Removed duplicated literal schemas and TypeScript-only unions.
- Added compatibility coverage for canonical settings and HTTP validation.

### Verification

- Contracts typecheck: passed.
- Contract settings tests: 2 passed.
- Config parsing tests: 8 passed.
- API schema tests: 2 passed.
- Desktop config/client tests: 25 passed.
- Changed-file lint, formatting, and `git diff --check`: passed.
- Final scan confirms the four literal sets exist only in the canonical module.

Full API-dependent typechecks/tests remain blocked by unrelated existing worktree errors, primarily the MCP `registerCycleMcpResources` initialization failure plus existing comment/page typing errors. No changed ticket files produced type diagnostics.
